command_title_to_documentation_map.add_annot_to_highlight:
# add_annot_to_highlight
Alows the user to select a highlight to add annotations to using the keyboard.
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_bookmark:
# add_bookmark
Add an invisible bookmark in the current location. You can later search the bookmarks using the [`goto_bookmark`](commands.md#goto_bookmark-goto_bookmark_g) command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_freetext_bookmark:
# add_freetext_bookmark
Add a freetext bookmark in the selected rectangle. Freetext bookmarks are visible in the document. You can change the display style of the bookmark using the following prefixes:

- if the bookmark text starts with `#` followed by a symbol, it will draw a box with the symbol's color (the same color as the highlighs of the symbol).
- if the bookmark text starts with `#markdown`, the rest of the bookmark will be rendered as markdown
- if the bookmark text starts with `#latex`, the rest of the bookmark will be rendered as latex
- if the bookmarks text starts with `? `, it will be interpreted as a question to be asked from the current document (using a large language model). The response will be displayed in the bookmark (this feature is only available to pro users).
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_highlight:
# add_highlight
Highlight the selected text. Requires a symbol which specifies the color of the highlighted text. For example executing `add_highlight` followed by pressing `a` will create a highlight with color `highlight_color_a`. You can configure the highlight color of all highlights in your config files. The highlights can later be searched using [`goto_highlight`](commands.md#goto_highlight-goto_highlight_g).
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_highlight_with_current_type:
# add_highlight_with_current_type
Highlight the selected text with the current highlight type as set by [`set_select_highlight_type`](commands.md#set_select_highlight_type).
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_keybind:
# add_keybind
Dynamically add a new keybinding.
command_title_to_documentation_map.add_marked_bookmark:
# add_marked_bookmark
Add a marked bookmark in the selected location. Marked bookmarks are visible in the document using a small icon. You can later search the bookmarks using the [`goto_bookmark`](commands.md#goto_bookmark-goto_bookmark_g) command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.change_highlight:
# change_highlight
Alows the user to change highlight type using keyboard. The user first enters the label of the highlight they want to change, then they enter the label of the new highlight type they want to change it to.
command_title_to_documentation_map.clear_current_page_drawings-clear_curren:
# clear_current_page_drawings, clear_current_document_drawings
Clear all freehand drawings in the current page/document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.close_window:
# close_window
Close the current sioyek window (doesn't close the application if there are more than one window open).
command_title_to_documentation_map.command:
# command
Open a searchable list of all commands.
command_title_to_documentation_map.command_palette:
# command_palette
Show a command palette with human-readable command names.
command_title_to_documentation_map.copy:
# copy
Copy the selected text to clipboard.
command_title_to_documentation_map.copy_drawings_from_scratchpad:
# copy_drawings_from_scratchpad
Copy drawings in the selected rectangle from the scratchpad to the main document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.copy_link:
# copy_link
Displays a symbol next to each visible link and copies the URL of selected link.
command_title_to_documentation_map.copy_screenshot_to_clipboard:
# copy_screenshot_to_clipboard
Copy a screenshot of selected rectangle to the clipboard.
command_title_to_documentation_map.copy_screenshot_to_scratchpad:
# copy_screenshot_to_scratchpad
Copy a screenshot of the document to the scratchpad (for example can be used to copy an image of an equation to the scratchpad).
command_title_to_documentation_map.create_fulltext_index_for_current_docume:
# create_fulltext_index_for_current_document
Add the current document to the fulltext search indexed. All indexed documents can later be searched using [`search_all_indexed_documents`](commands.md#search_all_indexed_documents).
command_title_to_documentation_map.create_visible_portal:
# create_visible_portal
Creates a portal with the selected window point as source. The source will be visible on the document. The destination of the portal can later be set using the [`portal`](commands.md#portal) command. An overview to the destination of the portal can be opened by right clicking on the portal icon.
command_title_to_documentation_map.delete_current_file_from_server:
# delete_current_file_from_server
Delete current file from sioyek servers.
command_title_to_documentation_map.delete_freehand_drawings:
# delete_freehand_drawings
Delete the freehand drawings in the selected rectangle.
command_title_to_documentation_map.delete_highlight_under_cursor:
# delete_highlight_under_cursor
Delete the highlight under mouse cursor.
command_title_to_documentation_map.delete_portal-delete_bookmark-delete_hig:
# delete_portal, delete_bookmark, delete_highlight
Delete the selected portal/bookmark/highlight. If no item is selected then we delete the closest portal/bookmark/highlight to the current location.
command_title_to_documentation_map.delete_visible_bookmark:
# delete_visible_bookmark
Delete a visible bookmark on the screen using keyboard by specifying the label of the bookmark.
command_title_to_documentation_map.donate:
# donate
Make a donation to support the development of sioyek.
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_paper_with_url-download_clipboa:
# download_paper_with_url, download_clipboard_url
Download and open a paper from an input URL ([`download_paper_with_url`](commands.md#download_paper_with_url-download_clipboard_url)) or the URL in clipboard ([`download_clipboard_url`](commands.md#download_paper_with_url-download_clipboard_url)).
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_link:
# download_link
Download a paper by selecting its link using the keyboard.
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_overview_paper-download_overvie:
# download_overview_paper, download_overview_paper_no_prompt
Downloads the paper currently shown in overview window. The [`download_overview_paper`](commands.md#download_overview_paper-download_overview_paper_no_prompt) command first displays the automatically detected paper title and asks for confirmation. The [`download_overview_paper_no_prompt`](commands.md#download_overview_paper-download_overview_paper_no_prompt) command downloads the paper without asking for confirmation.
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_paper_under_cursor:
# download_paper_under_cursor
Download the paper under mouse cursor. This can be the actual name of the paper (either in the document itself or the overview window) or a reference to the paper (e.g. executing [`download_paper_under_cursor`](commands.md#download_paper_under_cursor) while cursor is on a reference like `[12]` will download the paper referenced by `[12]`).
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_paper_with_name:
# download_paper_with_name
Download a paper given its name as input.
command_title_to_documentation_map.download_unsynced_files:
# download_unsynced_files
Download all files on sioyek server that are not available locally.
command_title_to_documentation_map.edit_portal:
# edit_portal
Edit the destination of the portal with the closest source. When [`edit_portal`](commands.md#edit_portal) is executed, sioyek jumps to the destination of the portal, now we can move around the destination and when we are done, we execute [`prev_state`](commands.md#next_state-prev_state) which updates the destination with the current location and brings us back to the source of the portal.
command_title_to_documentation_map.edit_selected_bookmark:
# edit_selected_bookmark
Edits the selected bookmark (a bookmark can be selected by clicking on it).
command_title_to_documentation_map.edit_selected_highlight:
# edit_selected_highlight
Edits the annotation of selected highlight (highlights can be selected by clicking on them).
command_title_to_documentation_map.edit_visible_bookmark:
# edit_visible_bookmark
Edit a visible bookmark on the screen using keyboard by specifying the label of the bookmark.
command_title_to_documentation_map.embed_annotations:
# embed_annotations
Embeds the sioyek annotations into the document as native PDF annotations. This is useful when sharing the file with others who do not have sioyek installed.
command_title_to_documentation_map.enter_password:
# enter_password
Enter the password for password-protected PDF files.
command_title_to_documentation_map.execute-execute_shell_command:
# execute, execute_shell_command
Executes the given shell command. The following substrings will be expanded in the command:

- `%{file_path}` will expand to the full path of current file.
- `%{file_name}` will expand to current file name.
- `%{selected_text}` will expand to the current selected text.
- `%{selection_begin_document}` and `%{selection_end_document}` will expand to the document location of the begin and end of current text selection. The format is `page x y`.
- `%{page_number}` will expand to the current page number.
- `%{command_text}` will ask the user for a text input and expand to the input.
- `%{mouse_pos_window}` will expand to the mouse position in the window. The format is `x y`.
- `%{mouse_pos_document}` will expand to the mouse position in the document. The format is `page x y`.
- `%{paper_name}` will expand to the current file's automatically detected paper/document title
- `%{sioyek_path}` will expand to the path of sioyek executable
- `%{local_database}` will expand to the path the local database file
- `%{shared_database}` will expand to the path the shared database file
- `%{selected_rect}` will expand to the selected rectangle in the document. The format is `page x1 y1 x2 y2`.
- `%{zoom_level}` will expand to the current zoom level.
- `%{offset_x}` and `%{offset_y}` will expand to the current absolute offsets
- `%{offset_x_document}` and `%{offset_y_document}` will expand to the current document offsets
- `%{line_text}`: if we are in ruler mode will expand to the text of current ruler line
command_title_to_documentation_map.execute_macro:
# execute_macro
Execute the given sioyek commands string. This is mainly used for scripting and it is probably not directly useful for end-users.
command_title_to_documentation_map.export:
# export
Export annotation data into a json file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.external_search:
# external_search
Search the selected text using the configured external search engine. This is a symbol command, and each symbol could be configured for a different search engine. For example in the default configuration `g` searches in google and `s` searches in google scholar. If no text is selected, we search the title of the current document instead (e.g. in the default configuration, to search the current paper in google scholar you can press `ss` when no text is selected).
command_title_to_documentation_map.extract_table-extract_table_with_prompt:
# extract_table, extract_table_with_prompt
Extract the contents of selected table.
The [`extract_table_with_prompt`](commands.md#extract_table-extract_table_with_prompt) command asks the user for a natural language prompt and extracts the table according to that (for example you can say something like this: "extract the contents of this table into a latex table").
command_title_to_documentation_map.fit_epub_to_window:
# fit_epub_to_window
Fit an epub document exactly to the window dimensions.
command_title_to_documentation_map.fit_to_page_width_ratio:
# fit_to_page_width_ratio
Fit to current document to a ratio of screen configured using [`fit_to_page_width_ratio`](configs.md#fit_to_page_width_ratio). This is useful for users with very wide screens.
command_title_to_documentation_map.fit_to_page_width-fit_to_page_height-fit:
# fit_to_page_width, fit_to_page_height, fit_to_page_width_smart, fit_to_page_height_smart, fit_to_page_smart
Fits the document to page width/height. The smart variants try to ignore the white page margins while fitting. `fit_to_page_smart` command fits the document to the minimum of width and height, ignoring the white margins.
command_title_to_documentation_map.focus_text:
# focus_text
Create the ruler under the line with the given text.
command_title_to_documentation_map.force_download_annotations:
# force_download_annotations
Download all unsynced annotations from the server. This is usually performed automatically but can be manually triggered with this command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.framebuffer_screenshot:
# framebuffer_screenshot
Take a screenshot from sioyek framebuffer (ignoring other widgets) and save it to the given path. This is mainly used for testing purposes.
command_title_to_documentation_map.generic_delete:
# generic_delete
Allows the user to delete annotations using keyboard. The user enters the label of the annotation they want to delete.
command_title_to_documentation_map.generic_select:
# generic_select
Allows the user to select annotations using keyboard. The user enters the label of the annotation they want to delete.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_beginning-goto_end:
# goto_beginning, goto_end
Move to the beginning/end of the document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_bookmark-goto_bookmark_g:
# goto_bookmark, goto_bookmark_g
[`goto_bookmark`](commands.md#goto_bookmark-goto_bookmark_g) opens a searchable list of current document's bookmarks. [`goto_bookmark_g`](commands.md#goto_bookmark-goto_bookmark_g) opens a searchable list of all documents' bookmarks.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_definition:
# goto_definition
Go to the the definition of the first symbol in the highlighted line using the @link(ruler_mode, "ruler").
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_highlight-goto_highlight_g:
# goto_highlight, goto_highlight_g
Open a searchable list of all highlights in the current document. You can limit the search to highlights of a certain type by prefixing the query by the type of the highlight followed by a space. For example `j {query}` searches for `{query}` only in highlights of type j. [`goto_highlight_g`](commands.md#goto_highlight-goto_highlight_g) opens a searchable list of all documents' highlights.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_left-goto_right-goto_left_smart-got:
# goto_left, goto_right, goto_left_smart, goto_right_smart
Move to the left/right side of the document. The `_smart` variants ignore the white page margins.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_mark:
# goto_mark
Goto a mark previously set by [`set_mark`](commands.md#set_mark). See [`set_mark`](commands.md#set_mark) for more details.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_next_highlight-goto_prev_highlight:
# goto_next_highlight, goto_prev_highlight
Jump to the next/previous highlight in the current document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_next_highlight_of_type-goto_prev_hi:
# goto_next_highlight_of_type, goto_prev_highlight_of_type
Jump to the next/previous highlight with the current highlight type (as set by [`set_select_highlight_type`](commands.md#set_select_highlight_type))
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_overview:
# goto_overview
Jump to the location of current overview.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_portal:
# goto_portal
Go to the destination of the portal with the closest source.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_selected_text:
# goto_selected_text
Jump to the location of selected text.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_toc:
# goto_toc
Open the table of contents.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_top_of_page-goto_bottom_of_page:
# goto_top_of_page, goto_bottom_of_page
Go to the top/bottom of the current page.
command_title_to_documentation_map.goto_window:
# goto_window
Open a searchable list of current sioyek windows.
command_title_to_documentation_map.import:
# import
Import annotation data from an exported json file using [`export`](commands.md#export).
command_title_to_documentation_map.import_annotations:
# import_annotations
Imports the native PDF annotations into sioyek annotations.
command_title_to_documentation_map.import_local_database-import_shared_data:
# import_local_database, import_shared_database
Import the data from `local.db` or `shared.db` file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.increase_freetext_font_size-decrease_fre:
# increase_freetext_font_size, decrease_freetext_font_size
Increase/decrease the font size of the freetext bookmark being edited.
command_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_overview:
# keyboard_overview
Displays a tag next to each word in the document, opens an overview to the selected tag's word (this works even for documents that don't have links, otherwise [`overview_link`](commands.md#overview_link) would be used).
command_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_select:
# keyboard_select
Displays a tag next to each word in the document, selects the text corresponding to the entered begin and end tag (separated by a space).
command_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_select_line:
# keyboard_select_line
Select a line using keyboard. Show a label next to each visible line and create a ruler under that line when a label corresponding to that line is entered.
command_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_smart_jump:
# keyboard_smart_jump
Displays a tag next to each word in the document, jumps to the selected tag's word (this works even for documents that don't have links, otherwise [`overview_link`](commands.md#overview_link) would be used).
command_title_to_documentation_map.keys_user_all:
# keys_user_all
Shows a list of all considered `keys_user.config` files, sorted by priority. All of these files are applied but the files with higher priority override the lower-priority files if they contain the same setting.
command_title_to_documentation_map.load_annotations_file-import_annotations:
# load_annotations_file, import_annotations_file_sync_deleted
Load the annotations from the file created using [`write_annotations_file`](commands.md#write_annotations_file). The `load_annotations_file` doesn't delete anything from the current file but the `import_annotations_file_sync_deleted` will overwrite the current annotations with the annotations in the file and delete any annotations that are not in the file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.load_scratchpad:
# load_scratchpad
Load a previously saved scratchpad state. This state should have been previously saved using [`save_scratchpad`](commands.md#save_scratchpad).
command_title_to_documentation_map.login:
# login
Login into your sioyek account.
command_title_to_documentation_map.login_using_access_token:
# login_using_access_token
Login to sioyek servers using previously obtained access token.
command_title_to_documentation_map.logout:
# logout
Logout from your sioyek account.
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_left-move_right-move_down-move_up:
# move_left, move_right, move_down, move_up
Basic movement in the current document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_left_in_overview-move_right_in_over:
# move_left_in_overview, move_right_in_overview
Move left and right in the overview (you can use the normal up and down movement commands to move up and down).
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_ruler_up-move_ruler_down-move_visua:
# move_ruler_up, move_ruler_down, move_visual_mark_up, move_visual_mark_down
Move the ruler up or down.
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_ruler_to_next_block-move_ruler_to_p:
# move_ruler_to_next_block, move_ruler_to_prev_block
Move the ruler to previous/next text block.
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_ruler_next-move_ruler_prev-move_vis:
# move_ruler_next, move_ruler_prev, move_visual_mark_next, move_visual_mark_prev
Move the ruler such that more content is visible. This is the same as [`move_ruler_down`](commands.md#move_ruler_up-move_ruler_down-move_visual_mark_up-move_visual_mark_down) [`move_ruler_up`](commands.md#move_ruler_up-move_ruler_down-move_visual_mark_up-move_visual_mark_down) commands when the ruler is entirely visible, but when part of the ruler is offscreen (e.g. in mobile devices with a small screen) this command moves such that the obscured part of the ruler becomes visible and only moves to the next line when the entire line has been viewed.
A red marked highlights the leftmost part of the next ruler position, so when you reach the red mark you can run [`move_ruler_next`](commands.md#move_ruler_next-move_ruler_prev-move_visual_mark_next-move_visual_mark_prev) and continue reading from the left side of the screen.
command_title_to_documentation_map.new_window:
# new_window
Create a new sioyek window.
command_title_to_documentation_map.next_overview-previous_overview:
# next_overview, previous_overview
Go to the next/prevoius possible overview desitnation. For example, when executing [`overview_definition`](commands.md#overview_definition) there might be more than one possible target. This command will jump to the next/previous target.
command_title_to_documentation_map.next_chapter-prev_chapter:
# next_chapter, prev_chapter
Go to the next/previous chapter in table of contents.
command_title_to_documentation_map.next_item-previous_item:
# next_item, previous_item
Go to the next/previous search result.
command_title_to_documentation_map.next_page-previous_page:
# next_page, previous_page
Move to the next/previous page.
command_title_to_documentation_map.next_state-prev_state:
# next_state, prev_state
Move forward/backward in history. Note that this history works even across files (i.e. if you go to a different file you can return to the previous file by executing [`prev_state`](commands.md#next_state-prev_state) or go back forward to the new file by executing [`next_state`](commands.md#next_state-prev_state).)
command_title_to_documentation_map.noop:
# noop
Do nothing (for example can be used to unbind default keybindings, by binding them to `noop` instead).
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_containing_folder:
# open_containing_folder
Open the folder containing the current file in file explorer.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_document:
# open_document
Open the operating system's file explorer to select a document to open.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_document_embedded:
# open_document_embedded
Opens an embedded file browser in sioyek (unlike open_document which uses the operating system's file browser).
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_document_embedded_from_current_path:
# open_document_embedded_from_current_path
Opens an embedded file browser in sioyek rooted at the current opened file's directory with the current file being selected.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_external_text_editor:
# open_external_text_editor
Open an external text editor to edit the current text input. For example you can open an external editor to edit bookmarks or highlight annotations. The external editor can be configured using [`external_text_editor_command`](configs.md#external_text_editor_command), the corresponding text input will be updated in sioyek whenever the file is saved.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_last_document:
# open_last_document
Opens the last document that was opened in the current sioyek window.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_link:
# open_link
Displays a symbol next to each visible link and jumps when user enters the link symbol.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_local_database_containing_folder-op:
# open_local_database_containing_folder, open_shared_database_containing_folder
Open the folder containing the local/shared database file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_prev_doc:
# open_prev_doc
Shows a list of previously opened documents.
command_title_to_documentation_map.open_server_only_file:
# open_server_only_file
Open a list of files that the user has previously uploaded on sioyek servers but are not available on the current device.
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_definition:
# overview_definition
Open an overview to the definition of the first symbol in the highlighted line using the @link(ruler_mode, "ruler").
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_link:
# overview_link
Displays a symbol next to each visible link and opens an overview to link destination when user enters the link symbol.
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_next_item-overview_prev_item:
# overview_next_item, overview_prev_item
Open an overview to the next/previous search result.
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_to_portal:
# overview_to_portal
Open an overview to the closest portal.
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_to_ruler_portal-goto_ruler_port:
# overview_to_ruler_portal, goto_ruler_portal
Open an overview/Jump to the portal on current ruler line.
command_title_to_documentation_map.overview_under_cursor:
# overview_under_cursor
Open the overview window to the location of reference under mouse cursor. This works even when the document doesn't have links.
command_title_to_documentation_map.portal:
# portal
Creates a new pending portal with the current location as source. If we already have a pending-portal, create the portal with the current location as the destination. As an example use-case you can create a portal from a part of text describing an equation or table to the equation/table itself, the contents of the destination portal can be viewed in a separate window using [`toggle_window_configuration`](commands.md#toggle_window_configuration) or an overview to see the contents of the portal in the same window using [`overview_to_portal`](commands.md#overview_to_portal).
command_title_to_documentation_map.portal_to_definition:
# portal_to_definition
Create a portal to the definition of the first symbol in the highlighted line using the @link(ruler_mode, "ruler").
command_title_to_documentation_map.portal_to_link:
# portal_to_link
Displays a symbol next to each visible link and creates a portal from the current location to the selected link's location.
command_title_to_documentation_map.portal_to_overview:
# portal_to_overview
Create a portal from the current location to the overview location.
command_title_to_documentation_map.prefs_user_all:
# prefs_user_all
Shows a list of all considered `prefs_user.config` files, sorted by priority. All of these files are applied but the files with higher priority override the lower-priority files if they contain the same setting.
command_title_to_documentation_map.regex_search:
# regex_search
Search the contents of current document using regular expressions. You can navigate the results the same way you would with [`search`](commands.md#search-chapter_search) results.
command_title_to_documentation_map.reload:
# reload
Reload all of the current file's data. This can be useful for situations where the file has been modified outside of sioyek, although sioyek will try to automatically reload the file if it detects changes on rare occasions it might fail to do so, in such situations you can use this command to manually reload the file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.reload_config:
# reload_config
Reloads the config files. Sioyek automatically reloads the config files when they are changed but in rare situations when it doesn't do so automatically (e.g. due to a permission issue), you can manually reload them using this command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.reload_no_flicker:
# reload_no_flicker
This is similar to the [`reload`](commands.md#reload) with the difference that here we don't immediately delete the previous renders (which causes the screen to flicker before the new document is rendered).
command_title_to_documentation_map.resume_to_server:
# resume_to_server
Resume the current file location to the last location in sioyek servers.
command_title_to_documentation_map.resync_document:
# resync_document
Re-sync current document's annotations with sioyek servers. This command is usually performed automatically but can be manually triggered with this command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_under_cursor-visual_mark_under_cur:
# ruler_under_cursor, visual_mark_under_cursor
Create a ruler under the line under mouse cursor.
command_title_to_documentation_map.save_scratchpad:
# save_scratchpad
Save the current scratchpad state to a file. This file can be loaded later using [`load_scratchpad`](commands.md#load_scratchpad).
command_title_to_documentation_map.screenshot:
# screenshot
Take a screenshot from sioyek window and save it to the given path. This is mainly used for testing purposes.
command_title_to_documentation_map.screen_down-screen_up:
# screen_down, screen_up
Move one screen up or down. The amount of movement can be configured using [`move_screen_ratio`](configs.md#move_screen_ratio).
command_title_to_documentation_map.search-chapter_search:
# search, chapter_search
Search the contents of current document. You can use [`next_item`](commands.md#next_item-previous_item) and [`previous_item`](commands.md#next_item-previous_item) to navigate search results. You can optionally specify a page range using the following syntax: `<begin_page,end_page>query`. The [`chapter_search`](commands.md#search-chapter_search) command automatically fills the page range with the current chapter's page range.
command_title_to_documentation_map.search_all_indexed_documents:
# search_all_indexed_documents
Perform a fulltext search in all documents indexed by [`create_fulltext_index_for_current_document`](commands.md#create_fulltext_index_for_current_document) or [`automatically_index_documents_for_fulltext_search`](configs.md#automatically_index_documents_for_fulltext_search).
command_title_to_documentation_map.select_current_search_match:
# select_current_search_match
Selects the current highlighted search result.
command_title_to_documentation_map.select_freehand_drawings:
# select_freehand_drawings
Select the freehand drawings in the selected rectangle. The selected drawings can be moved by dragging the selection rectangle.
command_title_to_documentation_map.select_ruler_text:
# select_ruler_text
Select the current line highlighted by ruler, you can use the up and down movement commands to move the selection and [`toggle_line_select_cursor`](commands.md#toggle_line_select_cursor) to toggle between begin and end of the selection. Exit the selection mode by pressing escape.
command_title_to_documentation_map.semantic_search:
# semantic_search
Performs a semantic search on the current document. This method produces a relatively lower quality result but costs a lot less than [`semantic_search_extractive`](commands.md#semantic_search_extractive).

Explanation of how this method works for more technical users: This method simply splits the document into paragraph-sized chunks and embeds each chunk using an embedding model. Then it finds the chunks with most similarity to the query.
command_title_to_documentation_map.semantic_search_extractive:
# semantic_search_extractive
Performs a semantic search on the current document. This method produces a relatively higher quality result than [`semantic_search`](commands.md#semantic_search) but is more expensive.
command_title_to_documentation_map.set_mark:
# set_mark
Set a mark in the current location so we can return to it later using the [`goto_mark`](commands.md#goto_mark) command. After executing `set_mark` command, sioyek will wait for you to press a symbol, which can be a lowercase or uppercase letter. Lowercase marks are local to the current file but uppercase marks are global. If you press a symbol that is already used as a mark, the previous mark will be overwritten. You can later return to the mark by executing `goto_mark` command with the same symbol.
command_title_to_documentation_map.set_select_highlight_type:
# set_select_highlight_type
Set the default highlight type. This is mainly used with [`toggle_select_highlight`](commands.md#toggle_select_highlight) and [`add_highlight_with_current_type`](commands.md#add_highlight_with_current_type).
command_title_to_documentation_map.set_status_string-clear_status_string:
# set_status_string, clear_status_string
Set the message in sioyek status bar. This message will be displayed until it is cleared using [`clear_status_string`](commands.md#set_status_string-clear_status_string). This is mainly useful for scripts and plugins to display messages to the user.
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_context_menu:
# show_context_menu
Show a context menu below the mouse cursor. The commands shown in the menu can be configured using the following configs (depending on the object under mouse cursor):

- [`context_menu_items`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
- [`context_menu_items_for_links`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
- [`context_menu_items_for_selected_text`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
- [`context_menu_items_for_highlights`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
- [`context_menu_items_for_bookmarks`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
- [`context_menu_items_for_overview`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_touch_draw_controls:
# show_touch_draw_controls
Show the freehand drawing menu for touch devices.
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_touch_highlight_type_select:
# show_touch_highlight_type_select
Show the highlight type selector for touch devices.
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_touch_main_menu:
# show_touch_main_menu
Show the main menu for touch devices.
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_touch_page_select:
# show_touch_page_select
Show the page selector for touch devices.
command_title_to_documentation_map.show_touch_settings_menu:
# show_touch_settings_menu
Show the settings menu for touch devices.
command_title_to_documentation_map.smart_jump_under_cursor:
# smart_jump_under_cursor
Go to the location of reference under mouse cursor. This works even when the document doesn't have links.
command_title_to_documentation_map.move_down_smooth-move_up_smooth:
# move_down_smooth, move_up_smooth
Basic movement in the current document. Holding these commands results in a smooth continuous movement in the document unlike [`move_down`](commands.md#move_left-move_right-move_down-move_up) and [`move_up`](commands.md#move_left-move_right-move_down-move_up).
command_title_to_documentation_map.start_reading-stop_reading:
# start_reading, stop_reading
Start reading from the current line highlighted by ruler using the local text to speech engine.
command_title_to_documentation_map.start_reading_high_quality:
# start_reading_high_quality
Start reading from the current line highlighted by ruler using the sioyek servers' high quality neural text to speech engine.
command_title_to_documentation_map.synchronize:
# synchronize
Synchronize a desynchronized file with the server. On rare occasions files might become desynchronized where sioyek can not automatically decide whether to upload annotations to sioyek servers or not. For example suppose you have two copies of the same file on two different devices and with different annotations, and then you upload one of them to sioyek servers. When the other file is opened on the other device, sioyek will not automatically decide to upload the annotations to the server (because the user might not want that, e.g. perhaps those annotations are very sensitive data that you do not want uploaded). In such situations the other file is in desynchronized state and you can use this command to synchronize it with the server. This command will upload the annotations to the server if they are not already there.
command_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_forward_search:
# synctex_forward_search
Given a latex file path and line number, performs a forward search to the corresponding location in the PDF document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_under_cursor:
# synctex_under_cursor
Perform a synctex search under mouse cursor (this works regardless of whether we are in synctex mode or not). The search command is configured using [`inverse_search_command`](configs.md#inverse_search_command).
command_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_under_ruler:
# synctex_under_ruler
Perform a synctex search to the location of the line highlighted by ruler.
command_title_to_documentation_map.sync_current_file_location:
# sync_current_file_location
Upload the current file location to sioyek servers (can later be resumed on other devices with @(resume_to_server) command). This command is usually performed automatically (only on files that have been uploaded to sioyek servers) but can be manually triggered with this command.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_text_mark-move_text_mark_forward-:
# toggle_text_mark, move_text_mark_forward, move_text_mark_backward, move_text_mark_forward_word, move_text_mark_backward_word, move_text_mark_down, move_text_mark_up
These commands are used to manipulate the text selection using keyboard. `move_{forward/backward}` commands move the cursor one character forward/backward and `move_{forward/backward}_word` commands move the cursor one word forward/backward, and `move_{down/up}` commands move the cursor one line down/up. `toggle_text_mark` toggles the position of cursor between the start and end of text selection.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_custom_color:
# toggle_custom_color
Toggle between light and custom color schemes. The background and text color of custom color scheme can be configured using [`custom_text_color`](configs.md#custom_text_color-custom_background_color) and [`custom_background_color`](configs.md#custom_text_color-custom_background_color).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_dark_mode:
# toggle_dark_mode
Toggle between light and dark color schemes.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_drawing_mask:
# toggle_drawing_mask
This is a symbol command which given a symbol `s`, toggles the visibility of freehand drawings with type `s`.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_freehand_drawing_mode:
# toggle_freehand_drawing_mode
Toggle freehand drawing mode. In this mode you can use the mouse to draw on the document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock:
# toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock
Locks horizontal scrolling. This is useful for touchpad or touch devices when you want to always keep the document centered in the screen.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_line_select_cursor:
# toggle_line_select_cursor
Toggle between begin and end of line text selection by [`select_ruler_text`](commands.md#select_ruler_text).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_mouse_drag_mode:
# toggle_mouse_drag_mode
Toggle mouse drag mode. When enabled, you can drag the mouse to move the document (instead of text selection).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_pdf_annotations:
# toggle_pdf_annotations
Toggles the visibility of PDF annotations (these are different from sioyek annotations, the PDF annotations are embedded in the PDF file itself).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_pen_drawing_mode:
# toggle_pen_drawing_mode
Toggle pen drawing mode. In this mode you can use the stylus to draw on the document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_presentation_mode-turn_on_present:
# toggle_presentation_mode, turn_on_presentation_mode
Toggle/turn on presentation mode, in this mode we fit each slide to the entire page and movement commands move to the next/previous slide.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_rect_hints:
# toggle_rect_hints
Shows the locations of touch mode shortcut rectangles and the commands that they perform.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_ruler_scroll_mode-toggle_visual_s:
# toggle_ruler_scroll_mode, toggle_visual_scroll
Toggles ruler scroll mode. In this mode mouse wheel moves the ruler instead of scrolling the document.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_scratchpad_mode:
# toggle_scratchpad_mode
Opens a scratchpad, you can use this scratchpad to draw or do calculations (this is mainly useful for touch/stylus devices).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_select_highlight:
# toggle_select_highlight
Toggle select highlight mode. In this mode all selected text will automatically be highlighted with the type set using [`set_select_highlight_type`](commands.md#set_select_highlight_type).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_synctex-turn_on_synctex:
# toggle_synctex, turn_on_synctex
Toggle/turn on synctex mode. In this mode, right clicking on the PDF file performs an inverse search from the corresponding location in the source latex file (using the configured [`inverse_search_command`](configs.md#inverse_search_command)).
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_titlebar:
# toggle_titlebar
Toggle the titlebar of the sioyek window.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_two_page_mode:
# toggle_two_page_mode
Toggles between two-page mode and single-page mode.
command_title_to_documentation_map.toggle_window_configuration:
# toggle_window_configuration
Toggle helper window. The helper window displays the portal destination of the closest portal source to current location.
command_title_to_documentation_map.turn_on_all_drawings-turn_off_all_drawin:
# turn_on_all_drawings, turn_off_all_drawings
Turn all drawings on/off.
command_title_to_documentation_map.undo_drawing:
# undo_drawing
Undo the last drawn freehand drawing.
command_title_to_documentation_map.upload_current_file:
# upload_current_file
Upload the current file into sioyek servers.
command_title_to_documentation_map.wait:
# wait
Wait for the given duration (in miliseconds). This is mainly used for testing purposes.
command_title_to_documentation_map.wait_for_renders_to_finish-wait_for_sear:
# wait_for_renders_to_finish, wait_for_search_to_finish, wait_for_indexing_to_finish, wait_for_downloads_to_finish
Wait for all pending renders/searches/indexing to finish. This is mainly used for testing and scripting (for example we may want to wait for renders to finish before we compare a screenshot to the saved screenshot).
command_title_to_documentation_map.write_annotations_file:
# write_annotations_file
Write the annotations of current file into an annotations file next to the current file. The annotations file will have the same name as the current file with a `sioyek.annotations` extension. This is useful when you want to share the annotations with others or move them to another computer.

Alternatively you can set the [`annotations_directory`](configs.md#annotations_directory) config to a directory path and the annotations file will be written there instead of next to the current file.
command_title_to_documentation_map.zoom_in-zoom_out-zoom_in_cursor-zoom_out:
# zoom_in, zoom_out, zoom_in_cursor, zoom_out_cursor
Zoom in/out in the current document. The `zoom_in_cursor` and `zoom_out_cursor` commands zoom in/out around the cursor position while the `zoom_in` and `zoom_out` commands zoom in/out around the center of the screen.
command_title_to_documentation_map.zoom_in_overview-zoom_out_overview:
# zoom_in_overview, zoom_out_overview
Zoom in/out in the overview window.

config_title_to_documentation_map.adjust_annotation_colors_for_dark_mode:
# adjust_annotation_colors_for_dark_mode
Automatically adjust annotation colors to be more appropriate in dark mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.align_link_dst_to_top:
# align_link_dst_to_top
Normally when jumping to links, we align the middle of the screen with the link destination. If this option is set we align the top of the screen instead.
config_title_to_documentation_map.allow_horizontal_drag_when_document_is_s:
# allow_horizontal_drag_when_document_is_small
Normally when the document is smaller than screen width, we center in the screen and disallow horizontal mouse dragging to keep the document centered. If this option is set, then we allow the document to be dragged even when it is smaller than the screen width.
config_title_to_documentation_map.allow_main_view_scroll_while_in_overview:
# allow_main_view_scroll_while_in_overview
Allow the main document to be scrolled when overview is opened (otherwise we use scrolling outside the overview while the overview window is opened to move between possible overview targets).
config_title_to_documentation_map.annotations_directory:
# annotations_directory
Normally sioyek keeps some annotations files next to the pdf files (e.g. the freehand drawings file). If you don't want this you can specify the [`annotations_directory`](configs.md#annotations_directory) and then sioyek keeps the annotations files in that directory.
config_title_to_documentation_map.autocenter_ruler:
# autocenter_ruler
Always center the document on the ruler when moving the ruler.
config_title_to_documentation_map.automatically_download_matching_paper_na:
# automatically_download_matching_paper_name
If this config is not set, when downloading papers, we search the paper name and show the user a list of matching papers and download it when the user selects one of the options. If this option is set and one of the found paper matches the query title exactly, we no longer show the user a list and automatically download the matching paper.
config_title_to_documentation_map.automatically_index_documents_for_fullte:
# automatically_index_documents_for_fulltext_search
Automatically index opened documents for fulltext search.
config_title_to_documentation_map.automatically_update_checksum_when_docum:
# automatically_update_checksum_when_document_is_changed
When the document is changed (e.g. a latex document being edited) automatically update the checksum.
config_title_to_documentation_map.automatically_upload_portal_destination_:
# automatically_upload_portal_destination_for_synced_documents
When uploading a document to sioyek servers, also upload the portal destinations for portals in that document.
config_title_to_documentation_map.auto_login_on_startup:
# auto_login_on_startup
Automatically login to sioyek servers on startup (if previous credentials are available).
config_title_to_documentation_map.auto_rename_downloaded_papers:
# auto_rename_downloaded_papers
If set, sioyek renames the downloaded papers to an automatically determined file name (e.g. instead of a cryptic name like `23434.234.pdf` it will be renamed to something like `some_paper_title.pdf`).
config_title_to_documentation_map.background_bookmarks_pixel_budget:
# background_bookmarks_pixel_budget
Maximum number of pixels allocated to cached freetext bookmarks.
config_title_to_documentation_map.background_color-custom_color_mode_empty:
# background_color, custom_color_mode_empty_background_color, dark_mode_background_color
The empty background color in light/dark/custom color modes.
config_title_to_documentation_map.case_sensitive_search:
# case_sensitive_search
If set, we match the search queries in a case-sensitive manner.
config_title_to_documentation_map.check_for_updates_on_startup:
# check_for_updates_on_startup
Check for software updates when sioyek starts.
config_title_to_documentation_map.shift_click_command-control_click_comman:
# shift_click_command, control_click_command, command_click_command, hold_middle_click_command, tablet_pen_click_command, tablet_pen_double_click_command, right_click_command, middle_click_command, shift_middle_click_command, control_middle_click_command, command_middle_click_command, alt_middle_click_command, shift_right_click_command, control_right_click_command, command_right_click_command, alt_click_command, alt_right_click_command
`macro` commands to run when the corresponding button is clicked (while possibly holding modifier keys). Example:

```
alt_click_command toggle_dark_mode;toggle_scrollbar;set_status_string('hi there')
```
config_title_to_documentation_map.collapsed_toc:
# collapsed_toc
Open the table of contents in collapsed state by default.
config_title_to_documentation_map.context_menu_items-context_menu_items_fo:
# context_menu_items, context_menu_items_for_links, context_menu_items_for_selected_text, context_menu_items_for_highlights, context_menu_items_for_bookmarks, context_menu_items_for_overview
Items in context menu to show when [`show_context_menu`](commands.md#show_context_menu) is executed and the cursor is on the object specific to the option ([`context_menu_items`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview) is for the case where no special object is under the cursor).

If [`right_click_context_menu`](configs.md#right_click_context_menu) is set, you can open this all these menus (except for [`context_menu_items`](configs.md#context_menu_items-context_menu_items_for_links-context_menu_items_for_selected_text-context_menu_items_for_highlights-context_menu_items_for_bookmarks-context_menu_items_for_overview)) by right-clicking on objects in the page.
config_title_to_documentation_map.create_table_of_contents_if_not_exists:
# create_table_of_contents_if_not_exists
If the document doesn't have a table of contents, try to automatically generate one (this is a heuristic and doesn't work perfectly all the time).
config_title_to_documentation_map.custom_text_color-custom_background_colo:
# custom_text_color, custom_background_color
Text and background colors in custom color mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.dark_mode_contrast:
# dark_mode_contrast
The color contrast in dark mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.default_open_file_path:
# default_open_file_path
The default root path when opening files.
config_title_to_documentation_map.document_location_mismatch_strategy:
# document_location_mismatch_strategy
Specifies what to do when there is a mistmatch between a local document and the last location uploaded to sioyek servers.
Possible values are:
- `local`: remain in the local position
- `server`: move to the server position
- `ask`: prompt the user to choose a position
- `show_button`: show a resume button in sioyek statusbar
config_title_to_documentation_map.dont_center_if_synctex_rect_is_visible:
# dont_center_if_synctex_rect_is_visible
When performing a synctex search, if the target is already visible on the screen we don't move the document to center on the target.
config_title_to_documentation_map.epub_css:
# epub_css
CSS to apply to epub documents.
config_title_to_documentation_map.epub_width-epub_height-epub_font_size:
# epub_width, epub_height, epub_font_size
Configure the appearance of epub documents.
config_title_to_documentation_map.exact_highlight_select:
# exact_highlight_select
When adding the selected highlight, add the exact highlighted text (otherwise we highlight an entire word event when a part of it is selected).
config_title_to_documentation_map.external_text_editor_command:
# external_text_editor_command
The command line program to use when running [`open_external_text_editor`](commands.md#open_external_text_editor) command. In this config, `%{file}` expands to the file that should be opened. For example:

```
external_text_editor_command nvim "%{file}"
```
config_title_to_documentation_map.extract_table_prompt:
# extract_table_prompt
The plain english prompt to use when extracting table data using a large language model with [`extract_table`](commands.md#extract_table-extract_table_with_prompt) command.

Example:
```
extract_table_prompt Extract the table in this image into a markdown table. Take your time and make sure the result is 100% correct. Split muticolumns into separate columns.
```
config_title_to_documentation_map.fill_textbar_with_selected_text:
# fill_textbar_with_selected_text
When opening the textbar (e.g. when executing [`search`](commands.md#search-chapter_search) or any other command that requires text input) automatically fill the input with the selected text.
config_title_to_documentation_map.fit_to_page_width_ratio:
# fit_to_page_width_ratio
The screen width ratio when using [`fit_to_page_width_ratio`](commands.md#fit_to_page_width_ratio) command.
config_title_to_documentation_map.flat_toc:
# flat_toc
Show the table of contents as a flat list instead of a hierarchial tree. This can improve the performance on documents with extremely large table of contents.
config_title_to_documentation_map.font_size:
# font_size
The font size for UI menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.freetext_bookmark_color:
# freetext_bookmark_color
Text color to use when adding new freetext bookmarks.
config_title_to_documentation_map.freetext_bookmark_font_size:
# freetext_bookmark_font_size
Font size to use when adding new freetext bookmarks.
config_title_to_documentation_map.fuzzy_searching:
# fuzzy_searching
(Deprecated) Whether we should employ fuzzy searching in menus. As we are using a fuzzy algorithm by default in more and more menus this option is becoming less relevant as time goes on.
config_title_to_documentation_map.gamma:
# gamma
The gamma value, each pixel value `p` will be replace with `p^gamma`. Higher gamma values make fonts look thicker and lower gamma values make them look thinner.
config_title_to_documentation_map.hide_overlapping_link_labels:
# hide_overlapping_link_labels
When highlighting links in a document with overlapping links, show the label only for one of the overlapping links (avoids clutter in some malformed documents).
config_title_to_documentation_map.highlight_color_[a-z]:
# highlight_color_[a-z]
Specifies the color for each of highlight types.
config_title_to_documentation_map.highlight_links:
# highlight_links
Whether we should highlight PDF links.
config_title_to_documentation_map.highlight_middle_click:
# highlight_middle_click
If set, middle clicking on selected text highlights it with the current highlight type.
config_title_to_documentation_map.hover_overview:
# hover_overview
Open the overview page when mouse cursor hovers over a link (I don't recommend using this config because it might not work well with other sioyek features, just right click on the link to create the overview).
config_title_to_documentation_map.ignore_scroll_events:
# ignore_scroll_events
Ignore scroll events (e.g. useful to avoid moving in presentation mode when accidentally touching the touchpad).
config_title_to_documentation_map.ignore_whitespace_in_presentation_mode:
# ignore_whitespace_in_presentation_mode
Ignore whitespace when fitting pages to screen in presentation mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.incremental_search:
# incremental_search
If this is set, then we perform searches as user is typing the search query.
config_title_to_documentation_map.initial_snapped_dragging:
# initial_snapped_dragging
If set, when dragging the document (e.g. in touch mode) we ignore very small horizontal scrolls because when the user is trying to scroll vertically they probably will also do some horizontal scrollling inadvertently. So we start the dragging in a "snapped" state (meaning we are snapped to the original horizontal position) and only unspan when the user moves horizontally past a threshold.
config_title_to_documentation_map.inverse_search_command:
# inverse_search_command
The command line program to open the corresponding file when peforming a synctex inverse search. `%1` will expand to the path of TeX file and `%2` will exapnd to the line number.

For example here is how to configure it for vscode on windows:
```
inverse_search_command      "C:\path\to\Code.exe" -r -g "%1":%2
```
config_title_to_documentation_map.inverted_horizontal_scrolling:
# inverted_horizontal_scrolling
Invert the horizontal scrolling when using touchpad.
config_title_to_documentation_map.invert_selected_text:
# invert_selected_text
Invert the selected text to highlight it.
config_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_point_selection:
# keyboard_point_selection
If set, when executing commands that require a point on the screen to be selected, we show a list of labels on the screen and allow the user to select the point by typing the label using keyboard.
config_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_select_background_color-keyboar:
# keyboard_select_background_color, keyboard_select_text_color, keyboard_selected_tag_background_color, keyboard_selected_tag_text_color
The color of the tags displayed for keyboard commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.keyboard_select_font_size:
# keyboard_select_font_size
The font size of the tags displayed for keyboard commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.linear_filter:
# linear_filter
Perform linear texture filtering instead of nearest neighbor (should not be necessary unless you use non-interger render scales).
config_title_to_documentation_map.link_highlight_color:
# link_highlight_color
Highlight color to use when highlighting links.
config_title_to_documentation_map.max_created_toc_size:
# max_created_toc_size
The maximum size of automatically generated table of contents.
config_title_to_documentation_map.menu_matched_search_highlight:
# menu_matched_search_highlight
The CSS style used to highlight matched search results in menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.menu_screen_width_ratio-menu_screen_heig:
# menu_screen_width_ratio, menu_screen_height_ratio
The ratio of screen width/height to use for menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.middle_click_search_engine-shift_middle_:
# middle_click_search_engine, shift_middle_click_search_engine
When middle clicking on a paper name, we will search for it in the engine (as configured in @config(search_url_[a-z])) set here.

For example:
```
search_url_s https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=
middle_click_search_engine s
```
means that we will search the paper name in google scholar when user middle clicks on a paper name.
config_title_to_documentation_map.move_screen_ratio:
# move_screen_ratio
The ratio of screen height to move when executing [`screen_down`](commands.md#screen_down-screen_up) and [`screen_up`](commands.md#screen_down-screen_up).
config_title_to_documentation_map.numeric_tags:
# numeric_tags
When performing `keyboard_` commands that require a point to be selected, we associate a tag with each possible target. If this option is set, the tags only use numerical values instead of alphabetical values.
config_title_to_documentation_map.num_cached_pages:
# num_cached_pages
Total number of cached rendered pages. Higher values may reduce flickering and power consumption (because pages have to be re-rendered less) at the cost of increased memory usage.
config_title_to_documentation_map.num_prerendered_next_slides-num_prerende:
# num_prerendered_next_slides, num_prerendered_prev_slides
Number of next/previous pages to prerender in presentation mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.num_two_page_columns:
# num_two_page_columns
Changes the number of page columns in "two" page mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.overview_reference_highlight_color:
# overview_reference_highlight_color
Highlight color to use when highlighting the automatically detected overview paper name.
config_title_to_documentation_map.page_separator_width-page_separator_colo:
# page_separator_width, page_separator_color
Color and size of separator between pages.
config_title_to_documentation_map.page_space_x-page_space_y:
# page_space_x, page_space_y
Spacing between pages in two page mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.papers_folder_path:
# papers_folder_path
Path of the directory where downloaded papers should move to.
config_title_to_documentation_map.paper_download_should_create_portal:
# paper_download_should_create_portal
When downloading papers, create a portal from the source document to the downloaded document. Otherwise (if this config is 0) we open the downloaded document in a new window.
config_title_to_documentation_map.paper_download_should_detect_paper_name:
# paper_download_should_detect_paper_name
When downloading papers, create a portal from the source document to the downloaded document. Otherwise (if this config is 0) we open the downloaded document in a new window.
config_title_to_documentation_map.prerendered_page_count:
# prerendered_page_count
Number of pages to pre-render.
config_title_to_documentation_map.prerender_next_page_presentation:
# prerender_next_page_presentation
Prerender the next page in presentation mode (avoids flickering when moving to the next slide).
config_title_to_documentation_map.preserve_image_colors_in_dark_mode-inver:
# preserve_image_colors_in_dark_mode, inverted_preserved_image_colors
Preserve original image colors in dark mode and custom color mode.
If [`inverted_preserved_image_colors`](configs.md#preserve_image_colors_in_dark_mode-inverted_preserved_image_colors) is set, then we invert the original image colors in custom color mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.question_bookmark_background_color-quest:
# question_bookmark_background_color, question_bookmark_text_color
The background and text color of question bookmarks (the bookmarks starting with `? `, see [`add_freetext_bookmark`](commands.md#add_freetext_bookmark) for more information).
config_title_to_documentation_map.real_page_separation:
# real_page_separation
The page separator configured using [`page_separator_width`](configs.md#page_separator_width-page_separator_color) doesn't actually separate the pages, it is simply a box drawn to distinguish the pages. If [`real_page_separation`](configs.md#real_page_separation) is set, then we actually move the pages to separate them (technically it is less efficient than the other method which is why it is not the default but it shouldn't matter in most cases).
Also note that when this option is set, the distance between pages is not configured with [`page_separator_width`](configs.md#page_separator_width-page_separator_color) rather it uses [`page_space_y`](configs.md#page_space_x-page_space_y).
config_title_to_documentation_map.reload_interval_miliseconds:
# reload_interval_miliseconds
We check the document for changes (e.g. a latex document might be changed from external sources) every `reload_interval_miliseconds`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.render_freetext_borders:
# render_freetext_borders
Render the borders around freetext bookmarks.
config_title_to_documentation_map.render_pdf_annotations:
# render_pdf_annotations
If set to 0, we don't render PDF annotations (by PDF annotations I mean the native PDF annotations embedded in the PDF file and not the sioyek annotations).
config_title_to_documentation_map.resize_command:
# resize_command
`macro` commands to run when sioyek window is resized.
Example:

```
resize_command fit_to_page_width_smart
```
config_title_to_documentation_map.right_click_context_menu:
# right_click_context_menu
Show a context menu when user right clicks on annotations/overview. The commands displayed in this context menu can be configured using @conf(context_menu_items*) commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_auto_move_threshold_distance:
# ruler_auto_move_threshold_distance
In touch mode, you are able to move the ruler by pressing on the move down rect and then moving the cursor, each time the cursor moves `ruler_auto_move_threshold_distance` we move to the next line.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_display_mode:
# ruler_display_mode
Specifies how the ruler should be rendered. The possible values are:

- `box`: draw a box around selected line
- `slit`: draw a shadow on everything except the selected line
- `underline`: draw a line under the selected line
- `highlight_below`: highlight the entire screen area below the line
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_marker_color:
# ruler_marker_color
The ruler marker color to highlight the next left side of the screen when using [`move_ruler_next`](commands.md#move_ruler_next-move_ruler_prev-move_visual_mark_next-move_visual_mark_prev).
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_:
# ruler_next_page_end_pos, ruler_next_page_begin_pos
[`ruler_next_page_end_pos`](configs.md#ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_begin_pos) determines when we should move to the next page when using the ruler and when we do so, [`ruler_next_page_begin_pos`](configs.md#ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_begin_pos) determines where the new location of the ruler should be in the screen.

[`ruler_next_page_begin_pos`](configs.md#ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_begin_pos) is relative to the center of the screen and [`ruler_next_page_end_pos`](configs.md#ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_begin_pos) is relative to the bottom of the screen. For example, [`ruler_next_page_begin_pos`](configs.md#ruler_next_page_end_pos-ruler_next_page_begin_pos)=1 means we move one unit (each unit is half screen height) up from the middle of the screen which means we move to the top of the screen when moving to the next page.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_padding-ruler_x_padding:
# ruler_padding, ruler_x_padding
Ruler padding when using `slit` ruler display mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_pixel_width:
# ruler_pixel_width
Ruler thickness when [`ruler_display_mode`](configs.md#ruler_display_mode) is set to `underline`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ruler_slit_color:
# ruler_slit_color
Ruler color when [`ruler_display_mode`](configs.md#ruler_display_mode) is set to `slit`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.save_externally_edited_text_on_focus:
# save_externally_edited_text_on_focus
If this option is set, when editing text using [`open_external_text_editor`](commands.md#open_external_text_editor) command, we accept the externally edited text when the sioyek window gains focus again (otherwise the user has to manually press enter to accept the input).
config_title_to_documentation_map.scrollview_sensitivity:
# scrollview_sensitivity
The relative sensitivity when scrolling the overview window.
config_title_to_documentation_map.search_highlight_color:
# search_highlight_color
Highligh color of search matches.
config_title_to_documentation_map.search_url_[a-z]:
# search_url_[a-z]
Search url for [`external_search`](commands.md#external_search) command. After executing external search, the user can enter the symbol corresponding to the configured search engine to perform the search using that engine.

For example if we have the following in `prefs_user.config`:

```
search_url_g https://www.google.com/search?q=
search_url_s https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=
```

Then executing [`external_search`](commands.md#external_search) followed by `s` will search google and if it is followed by `s` it will search google scholar.
config_title_to_documentation_map.server_and_local_document_mismatch_thres:
# server_and_local_document_mismatch_threshold
When the location of server and document do not match (for example when the user has moved the document to another location in another device) if the distance is greater than this threshold a resume button will be displayed in the statusbar which allows the user to resume to the server location.
config_title_to_documentation_map.shared_database_path:
# shared_database_path
Path of shared database to use, if not set we use the default path.
config_title_to_documentation_map.should_highlight_unselected_search:
# should_highlight_unselected_search
Whether we should highlight the found search results that are not the current search result.
config_title_to_documentation_map.should_launch_new_window:
# should_launch_new_window
When opening a new file from command line, use a new sioyek window if one already exists.
config_title_to_documentation_map.should_load_tutorial_when_no_other_file:
# should_load_tutorial_when_no_other_file
When there is no other file to open (e.g. due to sioyek being opened for the first time) show the sioyek tutorial.
config_title_to_documentation_map.should_warn_about_user_key_override:
# should_warn_about_user_key_override
Print a warning message in console when a key override is detected in `keys_user.config`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.show_most_recent_commands_first:
# show_most_recent_commands_first
When opening the command menu show the most recent commands executed in the current session first.
config_title_to_documentation_map.show_reference_overview_highlights:
# show_reference_overview_highlights
Highlight the reference paper name in overview window (this is the automatically detected paper name which will be downloaded with [`download_overview_paper`](commands.md#download_overview_paper-download_overview_paper_no_prompt) command).
config_title_to_documentation_map.single_click_selects_words:
# single_click_selects_words
Normally when selecting text using mouse, we select individual characters, unless the user has double clicked to initiate the selection, in which case we select entire words instead of individual characters.

If [`single_click_selects_words`](configs.md#single_click_selects_words) is set, then we select words even when user uses single-click to initiate text selection.
config_title_to_documentation_map.sliced_rendering:
# sliced_rendering
Normally we render entire pages as a whole. This can cause problems on high zoom levels on low-memory devices becasue a huge chunk of memory with the correct size might not be available. If [`sliced_rendering`](configs.md#sliced_rendering) is set, instead of rendering pages as a whole, we slice the pages into multiple strips and render each strip.
config_title_to_documentation_map.smartcase_search:
# smartcase_search
If set, we match the search queries in a case-insensitive manner if all the characters in the query is lowercase and otherwise we perform a case-sensitive search.
config_title_to_documentation_map.sort_bookmarks_by_location:
# sort_bookmarks_by_location
Sort bookmarks by location (instead of creation date).
config_title_to_documentation_map.startup_commands:
# startup_commands
`macro` commands to run then sioyek starts up. For example:

```
startup_commands toggle_dark_mode;toggle_scrollbar;set_status_string(hi there)
```

Will set the color scheme to dark mode and enable scrollbar and show a message in sioyek statusbar when sioyek starts up.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_color-status_bar_text_color:
# status_bar_color, status_bar_text_color
Text and background colors of statusbar.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_font_size:
# status_bar_font_size
Font size of the status bar.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_format-right_status_bar_forma:
# status_bar_format, right_status_bar_format, status_*_command
The items and format to show in the statusbar. The default value is:
```
[ %{current_page} / %{num_pages} ]%{chapter_name}%{search_results}%{search_progress}%{link_status}%{waiting_for_symbol}%{indexing}%{preview_index}%{synctex}%{drag}%{presentation}%{visual_scroll}%{locked_scroll}%{highlight}%{freehand_drawing}%{rect_select}%{custom_message}%{download}%{download_button}%{network_status}%{tts_status}%{tts_rate}

```
The [`right_status_bar_format`](configs.md#status_bar_format-right_status_bar_format-status_*_command) is similar, but determines what to display on right-aligned statusbar.

Here is what each of the `%{}` variables expand to:
- `%{current_page}` expands to the current page number
- `%{current_page_label}` expands to the the label of the current page (might be different from `current_page` for example some documents have pages labeled with roman numerals, e.g. XII)
- `%{num_pages}` expands to the total number of pages in current document
- `%{chapter_name}` expands to the name of current chapter
- `%{document_name}` expands to the file name of current document
- `%{auto_name}` expands to automatically detected document name
- `%{search_results}` expands to the search results (if there is a search active)
- `%{search_progress}` expands to the current search progress (if there is one in progress)
- `%{link_status}` specifies whether we are linking (that is the source of a [`portal`](commands.md#portal) has been set and we are waiting for the destination)
- `%{waiting_for_symbol}` specifies if a command is expecting a symbol to be entered
- `%{indexing}` specifies if the current document is currently being indexed
- `%{preview_index}` if there are more than one possible overviews available for the overview window, this option shows how many there are and what is the current index 
- `%{synctex}` displays if we are in synctex mode ([`toggle_synctex`](commands.md#toggle_synctex-turn_on_synctex))
- `%{drag}` displays if we are in mouse drag mode ([`toggle_mouse_drag_mode`](commands.md#toggle_mouse_drag_mode))
- `%{presentation}` displays if we are in presentation mode ([`toggle_presentation_mode`](commands.md#toggle_presentation_mode-turn_on_presentation_mode))
- `%{visual_scroll}` displays if we are in ruler scorll mode ([`toggle_ruler_scroll_mode`](commands.md#toggle_ruler_scroll_mode-toggle_visual_scroll))
- `%{locked_scroll}` displays if we have locked horizontal scrolling ([`toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock`](commands.md#toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock))
- `%{highlight}` displays information about the current highlight state ([`set_select_highlight_type`](commands.md#set_select_highlight_type)) 
- `%{freehand_drawing}` displays if we are in freehand drawing mode ([`toggle_freehand_drawing_mode`](commands.md#toggle_freehand_drawing_mode))
- `%{rect_select}` displays if a command requires a rectangle to be selected
- `%{point_select}` displays if a command requires a point to be selected
- `%{custom_message}` user message that can be set using [`set_status_string`](commands.md#set_status_string-clear_status_string)
- `%{download}` displays if we are currently downloading a paper
- `%{download_button}` a button that displays when the current overview is a reference to a paper, clickin on this button downloads the paper
- `%{closest_bookmark}` displays the closest bookmark to current location
- `%{closest_portal}` displays the closest portal to the current location
- `%{selected_highlight}` displays the comment of the selected highlight
- `%{mode_string}` displays the current mode string (this can be useful when defining `macro`s)
- `%{network_status}` displays the current network status
- `%{tts_status}` displays the current text to speech status
- `%{tts_rate}` displays the current text to speech speed
- `%{custom_message_a}` to `%{custom_message_d}` expands to a custom message which can be set using @config(status_custom_message_[a-d])

Note that clicking on many of these items performs a relevant command. For example clicking on the text expanded from `${current_page}` shows a page-selection widget or clicking on `%{chapter_name}` opens the table of contents. The exact command executed when some part of the statusbar is clicked can be configured using @config(status_*_command) config options where `*` can be any of the options described above. For example if we have the following in the `prefs_user.config` file:

```
status_num_pages_command toggle_dark_mode
```

then clicking on the number of pages in the statusbar toggles dark mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_custom_message_[a-d]:
# status_custom_message_[a-d]
Show custom messages which can be shown in statusbar if `%{custom_message_a}` to `%{custom_message_z}` is added to [`status_bar_format`](configs.md#status_bar_format-right_status_bar_format-status_*_command).
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_font:
# status_font
Font face to use for menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.strike_line_width:
# strike_line_width
The strike width when adding strike highlights (highlights with type `_`).
config_title_to_documentation_map.super_fast_search:
# super_fast_search
If set, we index the document so that searches can be performed instantaneously. Some other sioyek features work only if this option is enabled so I recommend to keep it enabled (the extra memory requirement due to this option being enabled is extremely small).
config_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_highlght_timeout:
# synctex_highlght_timeout
Highlight color to use when highlighting synctex search results. This only applies when [`use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line`](configs.md#use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line) is set to 0, otherwise we use the ruler to highlight the line.
config_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_highlight_color:
# synctex_highlight_color
Highlight color to use when highlighting synctex search results. This only applies when [`use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line`](configs.md#use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line) is set to 0, otherwise we use the ruler to highlight the line.
config_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_highlight_timeout:
# synctex_highlight_timeout
Duration (in seconds) to highlight the synctex line. If the duration is -1, the highlight will stay indefinitely.
config_title_to_documentation_map.table_extract_behaviour:
# table_extract_behaviour
What to do with extracted table. The possible values are:
- `bookmark`: Create a freetext bookmark with the extracted table
- `copy`: Copy the extracted table into clipboard
config_title_to_documentation_map.tag_font_face:
# tag_font_face
Font face to use for keyboard tags.
config_title_to_documentation_map.text_highlight_color:
# text_highlight_color
The highlight color of selected text (e.g. when user selects text using mouse or using commands like [`keyboard_select`](commands.md#keyboard_select)).
config_title_to_documentation_map.toc_jump_align_top:
# toc_jump_align_top
When jumping to a table of contents entry, align the top of the screen with the target location (instead of center of the screen).
config_title_to_documentation_map.touchpad_sensitivity:
# touchpad_sensitivity
Relative scrolling sensitivity when using touchpad or mouse wheel, this is a multiplier that modifies [`vertical_move_amount`](configs.md#vertical_move_amount-horizontal_move_amount) so the overal move amount when using touchpad or mouse wheel is `touchpad_sensitivity * vertical_move_amount`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.touch_mode:
# touch_mode
This config is suitable for touch devices, if set, many behaviours of sioyek change to better support touch devices. For exmaple dragging on the screen moves the document instead of selecting text and menus are now optimized for touch devices.
config_title_to_documentation_map.back_rect_tap_command-back_rect_hold_com:
# back_rect_tap_command, back_rect_hold_command, forward_rect_tap_command, forward_rect_hold_command, middle_left_rect_tap_command, middle_left_rect_hold_command, middle_right_rect_tap_command, middle_right_rect_hold_command, ruler_next_tap_command, ruler_next_hold_command, ruler_prev_tap_command, ruler_prev_hold_command
`macro` commands to run when the corresponding rectangle is tapped or held (only works in touch mode). You can view the location of these rectangles by executing executing [`toggle_rect_hints`](commands.md#toggle_rect_hints).
config_title_to_documentation_map.tts_rate:
# tts_rate
The text to speech speaking speed when using text to speech commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ui_font:
# ui_font
Font face to use for menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.portrait_back_ui_rect-portrait_forward_u:
# portrait_back_ui_rect, portrait_forward_ui_rect, landscape_back_ui_rect, landscape_forward_ui_rect, portrait_visual_mark_next, portrait_visual_mark_prev, landscape_visual_mark_next, landscape_visual_mark_prev, landscape_middle_right_rect, portrait_middle_right_rect, landscape_middle_left_rect, portrait_middle_left_rect
Configures the rectangles in touch mode. If the user taps on these rectangles, we execute the corresponding command as configured in [`touch_rect_commands`](configs.md#back_rect_tap_command-back_rect_hold_command-forward_rect_tap_command-forward_rect_hold_command-middle_left_rect_tap_command-middle_left_rect_hold_command-middle_right_rect_tap_command-middle_right_rect_hold_command-ruler_next_tap_command-ruler_next_hold_command-ruler_prev_tap_command-ruler_prev_hold_command).
config_title_to_documentation_map.ui_text_color-ui_background_color-ui_sel:
# ui_text_color, ui_background_color, ui_selected_text_color, ui_selected_background_color
Text and background colors of ui menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.unselected_search_highlight_color:
# unselected_search_highlight_color
The highlight color to highlight unselected search matches when [`should_highlight_unselected_search`](configs.md#should_highlight_unselected_search) is set.
config_title_to_documentation_map.use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line:
# use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line
Use the ruler to highlight the synctex search result (instead of highlighting it with a color).
config_title_to_documentation_map.use_system_theme-use_custom_color_as_dar:
# use_system_theme, use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme
Try to use system theme for light/dark modes (might not work in some operating systems).
If [`use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme`](configs.md#use_system_theme-use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme) is set, then we use the custom color mode instead of dark mode when system theme is set to dark.
config_title_to_documentation_map.vertical_move_amount-horizontal_move_amo:
# vertical_move_amount, horizontal_move_amount
The amount to move when executing movement commands (including mouse wheel).
config_title_to_documentation_map.wheel_zoom_in_cursor:
# wheel_zoom_in_cursor
When performing zoom using control+mouse wheel, zoom on the mouse cursor instead of the center of the screen.
config_title_to_documentation_map.main_window_size-helper_window_size-main:
# main_window_size, helper_window_size, main_window_move, helper_window_move, single_main_window_size, single_main_window_move, overview_size, overview_offset
Default size and offset of sioyek windows. the `single_*` is used when only the main sioyek window is visible (no helper window) otherwise we use the other values.
config_title_to_documentation_map.zoom_inc_factor:
# zoom_inc_factor
The amount to multiply/divide the zoom level when executing zoom commands. For example, if the current zoom level is 2 and `zoom_inc_factor` is 1.5, then after executing [`zoom_in`](commands.md#zoom_in-zoom_out-zoom_in_cursor-zoom_out_cursor), the zoom level will be `2 * 1.5 = 3`.
